what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies a shared Suite.capture_corpus seam writes lineage-gated corpus records and then embeds them into the correct vector space, that both the MCP capture tool and the bridge /api/cognition/corpus route call that same seam, and that non-embeddable projections raise while missing projections remain capture-only; it uses a deterministic stub embedder and inspects source bodies to enforce the single-source embed path.
observations: Uses tempfile.TemporaryDirectory to create an isolated FsStore per test section.
Defines a stub embedder that maps JSON-serialized outputs to fixed vectors and ignores transport/model/dim.
Inspects source code of mcp_face.server.capture, runtime.bridge do_POST, Suite.capture_corpus, and Suite._embed_text to assert call shapes and absence of forbidden calls.
Tests both batch MCP-style records and a manually constructed single-record bridge-style body.
Builds a separate 'topics' vector space so find_relations has a far-space inversion target.
Checks that vectors land at store.space_address(source_address, projection).
Expects ValueError for a non-embeddable projection but embedded=None with no error when projection is absent.
Counts global PASS/FAIL via a simple check helper and exits 0/1 accordingly.
standouts: Closes a specific gap where the bridge capture route previously wrote a corpus record without embedding by forcing both faces through Suite.capture_corpus.
Uses source-code inspection to prove both the MCP tool and the bridge route call capture_corpus and do not duplicate the embed path.
Distinguishes fail-loud non-embeddable projections from legitimate capture-only records with no projection.
conventions: Follows an acceptance-test pattern with global PASS/FAIL counters and a check helper.
Uses sys.path insertion relative to the test file location.
Uses a long narrative docstring to state the bug, fix, and test plan.
Uses inspect.getsource plus string filtering to remove docstrings and comments before scanning call sites.
concerns: Source-code string scanning is brittle: renamed variables, wrapped calls, or conditional calls can defeat the inspection checks.
Imports mcp_face.server and runtime.bridge at module level, coupling the test to their import-time behavior.
Relies on the registry containing at least one embeddable projection and optionally one non-embeddable projection.
The stub embedder proves code path only, not real embedding service behavior or failure modes.
notes: An acceptance test that locks the shared capture+embed seam and uses source inspection to keep both MCP and bridge faces on a single path.
questions: What exact exception type should capture_corpus raise for a non-embeddable projection?
How is lineage validation implemented inside capture_corpus?
What is the real embedder contract for transport, model, and dim?
How does the production bridge route assemble the single-record body?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the docstring matches the test sections: it verifies lineage-first writes, shared capture_corpus usage by both faces, end-to-end embedding keys, fail-loud semantics, and the no-action floor.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for capture and embed.
Acceptance testing with stubbed external dependencies.
End-to-end key consistency between write and read paths.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prevent the bridge capture route from silently skipping embedding by forcing both the MCP tool and the HTTP bridge through one shared capture+embed method, while keeping capture as a computation step that never triggers decisions.
novelty: It treats source-code inspection of two different call sites as an acceptance assertion, turning 'both faces use the same function' into a testable invariant rather than a code-review note.
contribution: A deterministic acceptance harness that documents and guards the capture+embed seam contract: lineage-first persistence, shared routing through Suite.capture_corpus, fail-loud projection errors, capture-only no-projection records, and the no-resolve/approve/dispatch floor.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for a shared capture-and-embed seam that verifies lineage-gated corpus writes, vector-space population at the same key find_relations reads, identical routing through Suite.capture_corpus from both MCP and bridge faces, loud failure on non-embeddable projections, and absence of action/dispatch calls in capture code.
intention_for_embedding: To close a silent-failure gap where the bridge capture route wrote corpus records without embedding by locking both MCP and bridge paths to a single capture_corpus method and making projection errors raise.

symbols.does: Prints pass/fail for a single assertion and updates global PASS/FAIL counters.
symbols.description: A tiny test helper used throughout the file; it prints an 'ok ' or 'XX ' line and increments PASS or FAIL so the suite can exit with the right status.

symbols.does: Builds a Suite over a temporary FsStore with the local nodes directory discovered.
symbols.description: Convenience constructor used at the start of each isolated test section to create a fresh Suite, NodeRegistry, and FsStore rooted in a temp directory.

symbols.does: Returns a deterministic embed function that maps inputs through a lookup table with a fallback default vector.
symbols.description: Factory for the stub embedder used in the tests; the returned function ignores transport, model, and dim and simply looks up each input in the provided table.

symbols.does: The actual embed function produced by _stub; looks up inputs and falls back to a default vector.
symbols.description: Inner closure returned by _stub; it is passed as embed_fn to capture_corpus and vector_index build_index to make the tests deterministic and independent of the real embedding service.

symbols.does: Returns the id of the first embeddable projection registered in the suite.
symbols.description: Test helper that asserts at least one embeddable projection exists and returns its id so the test can target a real embeddable space.

symbols.does: Returns the id of the first registered projection that is not embeddable, or None.
symbols.description: Test helper used to find a projection that should trigger the fail-loud path when passed to capture_corpus; returns None if every projection is embeddable.

symbols.does: Strips the leading docstring and every comment line from a source string.
symbols.description: Helper used before scanning source code for call sites so that mentions of function names in prose or comments are not mistaken for actual invocations.
